key_insight: Molecular dynamics shows that AlCoCuFeNi thin-film deposition on Si(100) first forms nanoscale clusters, then begins crystallizing after about 5 ns into a mixed multi-phase structure.
engineering_relevance: The results help guide high-entropy alloy thin-film processing by linking deposition time and cluster evolution to phase formation and lattice-scale structure on silicon substrates.
method: Embedded atom model for Al, Co, Cu, Fe, and Ni interactions
Lennard-Jones potential for alloy–silicon interactions and Stillinger-Weber potential for Si–Si interactions
50 ns molecular dynamics simulation with radial distribution function analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that crystallization starts near 5 ns when clusters reach about 2 nm, and by 50 ns the film contains FCC, BCC, HCP, and amorphous phases.
watch_item: The study is simulation-based with a specific substrate/potential setup, so phase predictions may depend on force-field choice and may require experimental validation.
paper_title: Deposition and Growth of the AlCoCuFeNi High-Entropy Alloy Thin Film: Molecular Dynamics Simulation
